content.text: Flickers (Tier 1) — Cantrips
1 AP, +1 Toxicity each. 2 per round maximum. The rift equivalent of flicking a lighter. Cheap, not free — every Flicker is AP you're not spending on a Surge or moving.

rows: Ranged attack, Medium range. 1d6 rift damage vs Evasion. Ignores 1 armor.
Ranged attack, Close range. 1d4 rift damage to up to 2 adjacent targets. Each target saves vs Reflex (DC 6 + Interface) for half.
Push one creature 1 tile or move one object (up to 10kg). No damage.
Detect all rift energy sources, hidden rift creatures, and Toxicity concentrations within Medium range for 1 round.
Remove 1 stack of Poison from yourself or a touched ally. Rift energy burns out mundane toxin.

content.text: Surges (Tier 2) — Combat Spells
2 AP, +1–2 Toxicity. Real power. Requires 1+ Rift augment. Single-target Surges roll Interface vs Evasion. AoE Surges auto-hit the tile; targets make a Reflex save (DC 6 + Interface) for half.

rows: Ranged attack, Medium range. 2d8 rift damage vs Evasion. Ignores 2 armor. The workhorse single-target nuke.
Ranged attack, Medium range. Interface vs target's Grit. 1d8 damage (ignores ALL armor) + Daze (-2 to target's next action). Only works on creatures with a nervous system.
Ranged attack, Medium range. Grit vs target's Grit. 1d8 damage. Caster heals for damage dealt (cannot exceed max HP). The only self-sustain in the Channeler's kit.
Ranged attack, Medium range. Interface vs Evasion. 1d10 damage + target is Immobilized for 1 round (can still act but can't move).
Lightning arcs between targets. Hits up to 3 enemies within Close range of each other. 1d6 rift damage each. Each target saves vs Reflex for half.
All enemies within Close range of caster take 1d6 rift damage. Reflex save for half. Point-blank AoE — the panic button.
Target a tile within Medium range. 2d6 rift damage to all creatures within 5ft of the tile. Reflex save for half. Grenade-equivalent but costs Toxicity instead of credits.
Create a 10ft×10ft fog zone at Medium range. All attacks through it take -3 accuracy. Lasts 3 rounds. Blocks line of sight for everyone.

content.text: Torrents (Tier 3) — Major Damage
3 AP, +2–4 Toxicity, HP sacrifice. You are burning your own body to fuel this. Requires 3+ Rift augments.

rows: Single target, Long range. Interface vs Evasion. 4d8 rift damage. Ignores ALL armor. The single hardest-hitting attack in the game at this tier.
Line attack — pierces through all targets in a straight line, Long range. 2d8 to each target hit. Interface vs each target's Evasion. Ignores 2 armor.

Single target, Medium range. Interface vs Grit. 3d8 damage (ignores ALL armor) + apply Burn 1d4 (1d4/round, 3 rounds). If target is already Burning, apply Meltdown instead: 2d6 immediate damage on top of existing Burn.

Area bombardment. Target a 15ft×15ft zone at Medium range. 2d8 rift damage to all creatures in the zone. Reflex save for half. Terrain in the zone becomes difficult terrain for 1 hour (dimensionally scarred).

Chain reaction. Target one creature at Medium range. On hit: 2d8 damage. The energy then jumps to the nearest creature within 10ft: 1d8 damage. Then jumps again: 1d6 damage. Up to 5 total targets. Each jump is automatic (no accuracy roll, Reflex save for half).

Rip a hole in reality. Create a 10ft×10ft rift zone at Medium range. Anything entering or starting their turn in the zone takes 2d6 rift damage (no save). Lasts 3 rounds or until dismissed. The zone is impassable terrain.
Self-buff. For 3 rounds: +3 armor, immune to Poison, melee attacks deal +1d4 rift damage. When it ends: -1 to all checks for 1 round (exhaustion).
Teleport up to Long range, passing through all physical obstacles. Arrive with +2 Evasion for 1 round. Repositioning without movement.

content.text: Cataclysms (Tier 4) — Nuclear Options

4 AP, +5–8 Toxicity, HP sacrifice, permanent Consequence. Once per mission. Requires 6+ Rift augments. The Consequence: Every Cataclysm inflicts a permanent consequence on the caster in addition to all other costs. This is the price of wielding reality-breaking power.

rows: Single target, Long range. Interface vs Evasion. 6d10 rift damage. Ignores ALL armor. If target survives: they are Staggered for 2 rounds and take Burn 2d4 (2d4/round, 3 rounds). This kills most non-boss enemies outright.

Single target, Medium range. Interface vs Grit. 5d12 rift damage. Ignores ALL armor. Target's current armor is permanently reduced by 2 for the rest of the mission (dimensional erosion). If the target dies, the body is gone — dimensional disintegration. No loot. No corpse.

Massive area. 20ft×20ft zone at Medium range. 4d10 rift damage to all creatures in the zone. Reflex save for half. Zone becomes a permanent rift scar — difficult terrain, +1 Toxicity/round to anyone standing in it, persists after combat.

Everything within 15ft of caster. 5d8 rift damage to ALL creatures (enemies AND allies — no friendly fire exception). No save. Caster takes 2d8 additional self-damage. Reality buckles. The area is dimensionally unstable for 24 hours — random minor anomalies, gravity fluctuations, temporal echoes. The scorched earth option.

content.text: Future Ideas (TTRPG Sandbox)

The explicit pattern catalog above is the foundation — deterministic, stateless, implementable in code. The following systems are preserved for tabletop play where a GM can interpret results, negotiate effects, and make judgment calls. These are NOT part of the video game implementation but remain available for TTRPG campaigns.
Improvised Channeling

Describe the effect you want. The GM assigns a tier equivalent, stat, and cost (standard for that tier + surcharge). You pay the costs and roll 2d6 + stat. 10+ = full effect, 7–9 = partial/twisted effect, 6− = misfire. Improvisation always costs more than a defined Pattern.

When to improvise: The situation demands something no Pattern covers. You're combining elements. You're pushing a Pattern beyond its design. The surcharge balances the flexibility. GMs: be generous — if the player describes something cool and pays the cost, make it work on a good roll.
Ritual Casting (Out of Combat)
Some effects are too complex for combat's pace. Ritual casting allows Torrents and Cataclysms to be cast outside combat with reduced risk and enhanced effect.
Requirements: 10 minutes of uninterrupted concentration per tier, appropriate materials (rift crystals, chalk, ambient rift energy — GM determines), casting check with advantage.
Benefits: HP cost halved (round up). Misfire table one tier lower. Effects can be enhanced (GM negotiates — wider area, longer duration, additional targets).
Costs: Full Toxicity. Full Consequences for Cataclysms. Interruption during ritual = automatic Misfire at pattern's tier + Toxicity paid.

Ritual uses: Ward a safe house. Seal a rift tear. Purify a contaminated area. Scry a distant location. Create a dimensional anchor. Enchant an item temporarily. The rift can do almost anything if you give it enough time and enough of yourself.
Overchannel (Push Beyond Limits)

Announce before any cast. Double all costs (Toxicity, HP, Consequences). Effect is dramatically amplified — double damage, double area, double duration (GM determines specifics). Automatic misfire check regardless of Toxicity level. If misfire triggers: consequences are tripled instead of doubled. Overchannel represents pulling more power than your body can handle. It's the “I don't care if it kills me” option.
Custom Consequence Table (Cataclysms)
GMs can replace the standard Consequences with narrative consequences that fit the situation:

rows: Permanent Scar. The energy tears through you. Standard Scar rules apply.
Stat Degradation. -1 to a random stat permanently. Your body is diminished.
Mutation. Gain a random mutation (roll on mutation table). Might be useful. Might be horrifying.
Dimensional Echo. A copy of you from another dimension appears. It's hostile, has your stats, and lasts 1d4 rounds. Fight yourself.
Rift Scar. The area is permanently marked. Rift creatures are drawn to it. The location is changed forever.
Memory Burn. Lose 1d4 sessions worth of skill progress (LP). The energy burned neural pathways. You forgot things you knew.
